summary: Stripe Checkout backend regression complete. 13/13 pytest cases passed covering: auth-gated checkout, server-side amount calculation (tampered amount ignored), partial-with-custom-amount, partial-default-25%, unpaid-status-creates-no-booking, idempotent re-polling, webhook route registration, legacy /api/bookings flow with idempotency, and coupon-not-redeemed-on-unpaid. DB inspection confirmed payment_transactions persist correct server-computed amounts (7398 full / 1850 custom partial / 1849.5 default 25% / 7388 with coupon=10) and booking_context (travelers/contact_info/coupon).
critical_code_review_comments: Security: server-side amount calculation in _compute_amount_to_pay correctly ignores any client-supplied 'amount' field — verified via DB inspection (tampered amount=1 persisted as 7398).
Idempotency: _create_or_update_booking_doc early-returns when status is in (payment_received|confirmed|ticketed|completed); double POST /api/bookings returns same booking_ref. Verified.
_finalize_paid_session is correctly idempotent: bails out when transaction.status=='completed' && booking_id present, before any booking creation or coupon increment.

Coupon redemption is guarded by transaction.coupon_redeemed flag + only runs in the 'paid' branch; unpaid polls do not increment usage_count (verified — coupon WELCOMETRAVO usage unchanged after unpaid session).
Webhook router at /api/webhook/stripe returns 200 with {status:'error'} when signature missing — route registered correctly (no 404).
Minor: payments.py line 100 sets currency='aed' lowercase to Stripe (Stripe accepts case-insensitive), but persisted transaction uses 'AED'. Consistent and correct.

Minor nit: /api/payments/stripe/status/{session_id} has no auth dependency — any caller knowing a session_id can read its status. For Stripe-managed test session IDs this is low-risk, but consider adding get_current_user there too if you want to lock it down.

Minor nit: stripe_webhook.py catches Exception and returns {status:'error'} with 200 — Stripe will not retry on 200, which is fine, but the playbook recommends returning 4xx for signature failures so Stripe retries on transient errors. Not blocking.

success_rate.frontend: N/A — frontend testing skipped per request

seed_data_creation: None — used existing proposal pool (picked first proposal with total_price>0, observed total_price=7398 AED). No coupons created; relied on existing 'WELCOMETRAVO'.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Stripe flow is fully wired and idempotent. /api/payments/stripe/checkout requires auth and ignores client-side amount field (computes from proposal.total_price + markup_land - discount_amount - coupon_discount, then * 0.25 for partial-default). DB persistence: payment_transactions has session_id, amount, final_total, booking_context{travelers, contact_info, coupon}. Booking is created only on first 'paid' status poll (via _finalize_paid_session → _create_or_update_booking_doc, shared with /api/bookings legacy wallet flow). Idempotency holds for both Stripe-success path and direct POST /api/bookings (same proposal returns same booking_ref). Webhook endpoint /api/webhook/stripe is reachable and registered. Real paid-session E2E was not tested (requires a Stripe redirect & manual card entry — out of scope for backend regression).
rca of the issue: No issues found. End-to-end Stripe regression behaved exactly as designed.
